g.data-narration: Welcome to Function Overloading in C++ - a key concept for compile-time polymorphism.
g.text: Function Overloading and Ambiguity in C++

g.data-narration: Function overloading allows multiple functions with the same name but different parameters, enabling polymorphic behavior.
g.text: Defining multiple functions with the SAME name but DIFFERENT parameter lists
Enhances code readability: meaningful names for similar operations

g.data-narration: Without overloading, you'd need different names like addInt, addDouble - confusing and hard to maintain.
g.text: Used in: Constructor overloading, Operator overloading, Class member functions

g.data-narration: The compiler uses parameter lists to distinguish overloaded functions. Return type alone cannot differentiate functions.
The compiler resolves function calls in a hierarchical order: exact match, promotion, standard conversion, user-defined.
g.text: Promotion: char→int, float→double | Conversion: int→float, int→double

g.data-narration: Type conversion ambiguity occurs when an argument can be converted to multiple parameter types with equal cost.
g.text: func(10L); // AMBIGUOUS! long could convert to int OR double

g.data-narration: Combining default arguments with overloaded functions can confuse the compiler about which function to call.
Overloading with reference and value parameters of similar types creates ambiguity for variables but not for literals.
g.text: Ambiguity Type 3: Reference vs Value Parameters

g.data-narration: Arrays decay to pointers in function parameters, causing ambiguity when both array and pointer overloads exist.
g.text: Ambiguity Type 4: Pointer vs Array Parameters

g.data-narration: Classes can have overloaded constructors and member functions, providing flexible object creation and operations.
g.text: Both constructors and regular methods can be overloaded based on parameter lists

g.data-narration: A practical example showing area calculation overloaded for circle, rectangle, and triangle.
g.text: double area(float b, float h, float) {

g.data-narration: Explicit type casting resolves ambiguity by telling the compiler exactly which function to call.
g.text: Resolving Ambiguity with Explicit Type Casting

g.data-narration: Constructor overloading allows creating objects in multiple ways, each with unique parameter signatures.
Key takeaways: overloading depends on parameter lists, resolution follows exact-promotion-conversion hierarchy.
g.text: ★ Solutions: Explicit casting, unique signatures, avoid combining default args with overloads

g.data-narration: Remember these exam tips: return type doesn't matter, promotion order is char-short-int-long-float-double.
